Dan tahukah kamu) maksudnya kamu tiada mengetahui kapan ia akan terjadi (boleh jadi hari kiamat itu waktunya) yakni terjadinya (sudah dekat)

ar: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَنَ ٱلۡكَـٰفِرِینَ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمۡ سَعِیرًا
ar_simple: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَنَ الْكَافِرِينَ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ سَعِيرًا

t.sahih-int: Indeed, Allah has cursed the disbelievers and prepared for them a Blaze
t.asad: Verily, God has rejected the deniers of the truth, and has readied for them a blazing fire
t.yusuf-ali: Verily Allah has cursed the Unbelievers and prepared for them a Blazing Fire
t.pickthall: Lo! Allah hath cursed the disbelievers, and hath prepared for them a flaming fire
t.arberry: God has cursed the unbelievers, and prepared for them a Blaze
t.hilali-khan: Verily, Allah has cursed the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a flaming Fire (Hell)
t.itani: God has cursed the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a Blaze.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, Allah has cursed the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a flaming Fire.
t.qarib: The unbelievers have earned God's anger, and He has prepared a Blazing Fire for them
t.wahiduddin: God has rejected those who deny the truth and prepared for them a blazing Fire
t.shakir: Surely Allah has cursed the unbelievers and has prepared for them a burning fire
t.haleem: God has rejected the disbelievers and prepared a blazing fire for them
t.maariful: Surely, Allah has cursed the infidels, and has prepared for them a flaming fire
t.khattab: Surely Allah condemns the disbelievers, and has prepared for them a blazing Fire
t.ghali: Surely Allah has cursed the disbelievers, and He has prepared for them a Blaze
t.irving: God will curse disbelievers and prepare a Blaze for them
t.iwq: Indeed, Allah has cursed those who disbelieve and prepared for them a Blaze

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Allah melaknati orang-orang kafir dan menyediakan bagi mereka api yang menyala-nyala (neraka),
t_id.sabiq: Allah telah mengharamkan orang-orang kafir dari rahmat-Nya dan telah menyediakan bagi mereka api yang menyala dengan dahsyat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah melaknati orang-orang kafir) yakni menjauhkan mereka dari rahmat-Nya (dan menyediakan bagi mereka api yang menyala-nyala) yaitu neraka yang keras apinya tempat mereka dimasukkan.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Allah melaknati orang-orang kafir dan menyediakan bagi mereka api yang menyala-nyala (neraka),

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن الله لعن الكافرين» أبعدهم «وأعدَّ لهم سعيرا» نارا شديدة يدخلونها
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَنَ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمۡ سَعِيرًا (سَعِيرًا: نَارًا مُوقَدَةً، شَدِيدَةَ الحَرَارَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah melaknati orang-orang kafir) yakni menjauhkan mereka dari rahmat-Nya (dan menyediakan bagi mereka api yang menyala-nyala) yaitu neraka yang keras apinya tempat mereka dimasukkan

ar: خَـٰلِدِینَ فِیهَاۤ أَبَدࣰاۖ لَّا یَجِدُونَ وَلِیࣰّا وَلَا نَصِیرࣰا
ar_simple: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۖ لَا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا

t.sahih-int: Abiding therein forever, they will not find a protector or a helper
t.asad: therein to abide beyond the count of time: no protector will they find, and none to bring them succour
t.yusuf-ali: To dwell therein for ever: no protector will they find, nor helper
t.pickthall: Wherein they will abide for ever. They will find (then) no protecting friend nor helper
t.arberry: therein to dwell for ever; they shall find neither protector nor helper
t.hilali-khan: Wherein they will abide for ever, and they will find neither a Wali (a protector) nor a helper
t.itani: Dwelling therein forever, not finding a protector or a savior.
t.mubarakpuri: Wherein they will abide forever, and they will find neither a a protector nor a helper.
t.qarib: There they will stay forever, where they will find no protector or helper
t.wahiduddin: There they will live forever, and they will find therein neither friend nor helper
t.shakir: To abide therein for a long time; they shall not find a protector or a helper
t.haleem: There they will stay permanently, with no one to befriend or support them
t.maariful: wherein they will live forever, finding no one to protect or to help
t.khattab: to stay there for ever and ever—never will they find any protector or helper
t.ghali: Eternally (abiding) therein forever; they will find neither a patron nor a ready vindicator
t.irving: to remain in forever. They will not find any patron nor supporter
t.iwq: Abiding therein forever, they will not find a protector or a helper

t_id.kemenag: mereka kekal di dalamnya selama-lamanya; mereka tidak memperoleh seorang pelindungpun dan tidak (pula) seorang penolong.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka tidak akan pernah bisa beranjak dari neraka selamanya. Mereka tidak akan pernah mendapatkan pelindung atau orang yang bisa menghindarkan mereka dari siksa.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka kekal) dipastikan kekekalan mereka (di dalamnya selama-lamanya, mereka tidak memperoleh seorang pelindung pun) yang memelihara mereka dari neraka (dan tidak pula seorang penolong) yang dapat mencegah neraka daripada mereka.
t_id.iwq: mereka kekal di dalamnya selama-lamanya; mereka tidak memperoleh seorang pelindungpun dan tidak (pula) seorang penolong.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «خالدين» مقدرا خلودهم «فيها أبدا لا يجدون وليا» يحفظهم عنها «ولا نصيرا» يدفعها عنهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدٗاۖ لَّا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيّٗا وَلَا نَصِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka kekal) dipastikan kekekalan mereka (di dalamnya selama-lamanya, mereka tidak memperoleh seorang pelindung pun) yang memelihara mereka dari neraka (dan tidak pula seorang penolong) yang dapat mencegah neraka daripada mereka

ar: یَوۡمَ تُقَلَّبُ وُجُوهُهُمۡ فِی ٱلنَّارِ یَقُولُونَ یَـٰلَیۡتَنَاۤ أَطَعۡنَا ٱللَّهَ وَأَطَعۡنَا ٱلرَّسُولَا۠
ar_simple: يَوْمَ تُقَلَّبُ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِي النَّارِ يَقُولُونَ يَا لَيْتَنَا أَطَعْنَا اللَّهَ وَأَطَعْنَا الرَّسُولَا

t.sahih-int: The Day their faces will be turned about in the Fire, they will say, "How we wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger
t.asad: On the Day when their faces shall be tossed about in the fire, they will exclaim, “Oh, would that we had paid heed unto God, and paid heed unto the Apostle!”
t.yusuf-ali: The Day that their faces will be turned upside down in the Fire, they will say: "Woe to us! Would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger
t.pickthall: On the day when their faces are turned over in the Fire, they say: Oh, would that we had obeyed Allah and had obeyed His messenger
t.arberry: Upon the day when their faces are turned about in the Fire they shall say, 'Ah, would we had obeyed God and the Messenger
t.hilali-khan: On the Day when their faces will be turned over in the Fire, they will say: "Oh, would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger (Muhammad SAW)
t.itani: The Day when their faces are flipped into the Fire, they will say, “If only we had obeyed God and obeyed the Messenger.”
t.mubarakpuri: On the Day when their faces will be turned over in the Fire, they will say: "Oh, would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger."
t.qarib: On the Day, their faces will be turned about in the Fire, and they will say, "How we wish we had obeyed God and obeyed the Messenger
t.wahiduddin: On the Day when their faces are turned over in the Fire, they shall say: "Oh, would that we had obeyed God, and obeyed the Messenger
t.shakir: On the day when their faces shall be turned back into the fire, they shall say: O would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Apostle
t.haleem: On the Day when their faces are being turned about in the Fire, they will say, ‘If only we had obeyed God and the Messenger,’
t.maariful: The Day their faces will be rolled in the fire, they will say, “Oh, would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger!”
t.khattab: On the Day their faces are ˹constantly˺ flipped in the Fire, they will cry, “Oh! If only we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger!”
t.ghali: On the Day when their faces are turned about in the Fire, they will say, "O, would that we had obeyed Allah and had obeyed the Messenger
t.irving: on a day when their faces will be twisted by the Fire, they will say: "If we had only obeyed God and obeyed the Messenger
t.iwq: The Day their faces will be turned about in the Fire, they will say, "How we wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger

t_id.kemenag: Pada hari ketika muka mereka dibolak-balikan dalam neraka, mereka berkata: "Alangkah baiknya, andaikata kami taat kepada Allah dan taat (pula) kepada Rasul".
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari ketika wajah mereka dibolak-balikkan di atas api. Dan dengan penuh rasa sesal, mereka mengatakan, "Alangkah baiknya, kalau dulu kami menaati Allah dan Rasul-Nya."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari ketika muka mereka dibolak-balikkan dalam neraka, mereka berkata, "Aduhai!) lafal Yaa menunjukkan makna Tanbih atau memohon perhatian (andai kata kami taat kepada Allah dan taat pula kepada Rasul.")
t_id.iwq: Pada hari ketika muka mereka dibolak-balikan dalam neraka, mereka berkata: "Alangkah baiknya, andaikata kami taat kepada Allah dan taat (pula) kepada Rasul".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يوم تُقلب وجوههم في النار يقولون يا» للتنبيه «ليتنا أطعنا الله وأطعنا الرسولا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَوۡمَ تُقَلَّبُ وُجُوهُهُمۡ فِي ٱلنَّارِ يَقُولُونَ يَٰلَيۡتَنَآ أَطَعۡنَا ٱللَّهَ وَأَطَعۡنَا ٱلرَّسُولَا۠
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari ketika muka mereka dibolak-balikkan dalam neraka, mereka berkata, "Aduhai!) lafal Yaa menunjukkan makna Tanbih atau memohon perhatian (andai kata kami taat kepada Allah dan taat pula kepada Rasul)

ar: وَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَاۤ إِنَّاۤ أَطَعۡنَا سَادَتَنَا وَكُبَرَاۤءَنَا فَأَضَلُّونَا ٱلسَّبِیلَا۠
ar_simple: وَقَالُوا رَبَّنَا إِنَّا أَطَعْنَا سَادَتَنَا وَكُبَرَاءَنَا فَأَضَلُّونَا السَّبِيلَا

t.sahih-int: And they will say, "Our Lord, indeed we obeyed our masters and our dignitaries, and they led us astray from the [right] way
t.asad: And they will say: “O our Sustainer! Behold, we paid heed unto our leaders and our great men, and it is they who have led us astray from the right path
t.yusuf-ali: And they would say: "Our Lord! We obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they misled us as to the (right) Path
t.pickthall: And they say: Our Lord! Lo! we obeyed our princes and great men, and they misled us from the Way
t.arberry: They shall say, 'Our Lord, we obeyed our chiefs and great ones, and they led us astray from the way
t.hilali-khan: And they will say: "Our Lord! Verily, we obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they misled us from the (Right) Way
t.itani: And they will say, “Lord, we have obeyed our superiors and our dignitaries, but they led us away from the way.
t.mubarakpuri: And they will say: "Our Lord! Verily, we obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they misled us from the way."
t.qarib: And they will say, "Our Lord, we obeyed our masters and our dignitaries, and they led us down the wrong path
t.wahiduddin: They shall say, "Our Lord, we paid heed to our leaders and our elders, but they led us away from the right path
t.shakir: And they shall say: O our Lord! surely we obeyed our leaders and our great men, so they led us astray from the path
t.haleem: and ‘Lord! We obeyed our masters and our chiefs, and they led us astray
t.maariful: And they will say, “Our Lord, we obeyed our chiefs and our elders, and they made us to go astray from the path
t.khattab: And they will say, “Our Lord! We obeyed our leaders and elite, but they led us astray from the ˹Right˺ Way
t.ghali: And they will say, "Our Lord, surely we obeyed our masters and our great ones, so they made us err away from the way
t.irving: They will (also) say: "Our Lord, we have obeyed our superiors and our great men, and they led us off from the Way
t.iwq: And they will say, "Our Lord, indeed we obeyed our masters and our dignitaries, and they led us astray from the right way

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka berkata: "Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya kami telah mentaati pemimpin-pemimpin dan pembesar-pembesar kami, lalu mereka menyesatkan kami dari jalan (yang benar).
t_id.sabiq: Mereka memohon dengan mengatakan, "Wahai Tuhan, kami hanya mengikuti para pemimpin dan pembesar kami dalam usaha mengingkari-Mu dan Rasul-Mu, lalu mereka menjauhkan kami dari jalan yang lurus.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) yakni para pengikut dari kalangan mereka, ("Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya kami telah menaati pemimpin-pemimpin kami) menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Saadatanaa, dalam bentuk Jam'ul Jam'i (dan pembesar-pembesar kami, lalu mereka menyesatkan kami dari jalan petunjuk) dari jalan hidayah.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka berkata: "Ya Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya kami telah mentaati pemimpin-pemimpin dan pembesar-pembesar kami, lalu mereka menyesatkan kami dari jalan (yang benar).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقالوا» أي الأتباع منهم «ربنا إنا أطعنا سادتنا» وفي قراءة ساداتنا، جمع الجمع «وكبراءنا فأضلونا السبيلا» طريق الهدى
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالُواْ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّآ أَطَعۡنَا سَادَتَنَا وَكُبَرَآءَنَا فَأَضَلُّونَا ٱلسَّبِيلَا۠ (السَّبِيلَا: طَرِيقَ الهُدَى)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) yakni para pengikut dari kalangan mereka, ("Ya Rabb kami! Sesungguhnya kami telah menaati pemimpin-pemimpin kami) menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Saadatanaa, dalam bentuk Jam'ul Jam'i (dan pembesar-pembesar kami, lalu mereka menyesatkan kami dari jalan petunjuk) dari jalan hidayah

ar: رَبَّنَاۤ ءَاتِهِمۡ ضِعۡفَیۡنِ مِنَ ٱلۡعَذَابِ وَٱلۡعَنۡهُمۡ لَعۡنࣰا كَبِیرࣰا
ar_simple: رَبَّنَا آتِهِمْ ضِعْفَيْنِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ وَالْعَنْهُمْ لَعْنًا كَبِيرًا

t.sahih-int: Our Lord, give them double the punishment and curse them with a great curse
t.asad: O our Sustainer! Give them double suffering, and banish them utterly from Thy grace!”
t.yusuf-ali: Our Lord! Give them double Penalty and curse them with a very great Curse
t.pickthall: Our Lord! Oh, give them double torment and curse them with a mighty curse
t.arberry: Our Lord, give them chastisement twofold, and curse them with a mighty curse
t.hilali-khan: Our Lord! give them double torment and curse them with a mighty curse
t.itani: Lord, give them double the punishment, and curse them with a great curse.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Our Lord! Give them double torment and curse them with a mighty curse!"
t.qarib: Our Lord, give them double the punishment and curse them in a huge way
t.wahiduddin: Our Lord, give them double punishment and curse them with a mighty curse
t.shakir: O our Lord! give them a double punishment and curse them with a great curse
t.haleem: Lord! Give them a double punishment and reject them completely.’
t.maariful: Our Lord, give them twice the punishment, and send a curse on them, an enormous curse
t.khattab: Our Lord! Give them double ˹our˺ punishment, and condemn them tremendously.”
t.ghali: Our Lord, bring them torment twofold, and curse them with a great curse
t.irving: Our Lord, give them double torment and curse them with a great curse
t.iwq: Our Lord, give them double the punishment and curse them with a great curse

t_id.kemenag: Ya Tuhan kami, timpakanlah kepada mereka azab dua kali lipat dan kutuklah mereka dengan kutukan yang besar".
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Tuhan kami, lipatgandakanlah siksaan mereka. Usirlah mereka dari rahmat-Mu sejauh-jauhnya, sebanding dengan kejahatan yang telah mereka perbuat."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Timpakanlah kepada mereka azab dua kali lipat) daripada azab yang kami terima (dan kutuklah mereka) azablah mereka terus (dengan kutukan yang banyak.") bilangannya; dan menurut Qiraat lain lafal Kabiiran dibaca Katsiiran yang artinya kutukan yang besar.
t_id.iwq: Ya Tuhan kami, timpakanlah kepada mereka azab doa kali lipat dan kutuklah mereka dengan kutukan yang besar".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ربنا آتهم ضعفين من العذاب» أي مثليْ عذابنا «والعنهم» عذبهم «لعنا كثيرا» عدده، وفي قراءة بالموحدة، أي عظيماً
tafsir.siraj-ar: رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِهِمۡ ضِعۡفَيۡنِ مِنَ ٱلۡعَذَابِ وَٱلۡعَنۡهُمۡ لَعۡنٗا كَبِيرٗا (ضِعْفَيْنِ: مِثْلَيْنِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ya Rabb kami! Timpakanlah kepada mereka azab dua kali lipat) daripada azab yang kami terima (dan kutuklah mereka) azablah mereka terus (dengan kutukan yang banyak.") bilangannya; dan menurut Qiraat lain lafal Kabiiran dibaca Katsiiran yang artinya kutukan yang besar

ar: یَـٰۤأَیُّهَا ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِینَ ءَاذَوۡا۟ مُوسَىٰ فَبَرَّأَهُ ٱللَّهُ مِمَّا قَالُوا۟ۚ وَكَانَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَجِیهࣰا
ar_simple: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ آذَوْا مُوسَىٰ فَبَرَّأَهُ اللَّهُ مِمَّا قَالُوا ۚ وَكَانَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ وَجِيهًا

t.sahih-int: O you who have believed, be not like those who abused Moses; then Allah cleared him of what they said. And he, in the sight of Allah, was distinguished
t.asad: O YOU who have attained to faith! Be not like those [children of Israel] who gave offence to Moses, and [remember that] God showed him to be innocent of whatever they alleged [against him or demanded of him]: for of great honour was he in the sight of God
t.yusuf-ali: O ye who believe! Be ye not like those who vexed and insulted Moses, but Allah cleared him of the (calumnies) they had uttered: and he was honourable in Allah's sight
t.pickthall: O ye who believe! Be not as those who slandered Moses, but Allah proved his innocence of that which they alleged, and he was well esteemed in Allah's sight
t.arberry: O believers, be not as those who hurt Moses, but God declared him quit of what they said, and he was high honoured with God
t.hilali-khan: O you who believe! Be not like those who annoyed Musa (Moses), but Allah cleared him of that which they alleged, and he was honourable before Allah
t.itani: O you who believe! Do not be like those who abused Moses; but God cleared him of what they said. He was distinguished with God.
t.mubarakpuri: O you who believe! Be not like those who annoyed Musa, but Allah cleared him of that which they alleged, and he was honorable before Allah.
t.qarib: Believers, do not be like those who slandered Moses, but God cleared him of what they said, and that he was distinguished in God's sight
t.wahiduddin: Believers, do not behave like those who slandered Moses. God cleared him of their allegations. He was honourable in the sight of God
t.shakir: O you who believe! be not like those who spoke evil things of Musa, but Allah cleared him of what they said, and he was worthy of regard with Allah
t.haleem: Believers, do not be like those who insulted Moses- God cleared him of their allegations and he was highly honoured in God’s eyes
t.maariful: O you who believe, be not like those who annoyed Mūsā, then Allah cleared him of what they alleged; and he was honorable in the sight of Allah
t.khattab: O believers! Do not be like those who slandered Moses, but Allah cleared him of what they said. And he was honourable in the sight of Allah
t.ghali: O you who have believed, do not be as the ones who hurt Musa, (Moses) yet Allah (declared) him quit of what they said, and he has been esteemed in the Providence of Allah
t.irving: You who believe, do not be like those who abused Moses. cleared him of what they said. He became outstanding with God
t.iwq: O you who believe, be not like those who abused Moses; then Allah cleared him of what they said. And he, in the sight of Allah, was distinguished

t_id.kemenag: Hai orang-orang yang beriman, janganlah kamu menjadi seperti orang-orang yang menyakiti Musa; maka Allah membersihkannya dari tuduhan-tuduhan yang mereka katakan. Dan adalah dia seorang yang mempunyai kedudukan terhormat di sisi Allah.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai orang-orang beriman, jangan menyakiti hati rasul dengan cara apa pun sebagaimana perlakuan yang diterima Mûsâ dari kaumnya sendiri. Allah telah melepaskan keburukan-keburukan yang telah mereka sandangkan pada diri Mûsâ. Sesungguhnya Mûsâ adalah seorang yang berkedudukan tinggi di sisi Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Hai orang-orang yang beriman! Janganlah kalian menjadi) bersama Nabi kalian (seperti orang-orang yang menyakiti Musa) seperti perkataan mereka kepada Nabi Musa, "Sesungguhnya tidak ada yang mencegahnya untuk mandi bersama kita melainkan karena pelirnya burut (maka Allah membersihkannya dari tuduhan-tuduhan yang mereka katakan) yaitu pada suatu hari Nabi Musa ketika hendak mandi meletakkan pakaiannya pada sebuah batu, kemudian batu itu membawa lari pakaiannya, dan berhenti di tengah-tengah segolongan kaum Bani Israel. Lalu Nabi Musa mengejarnya dan dapat menyusulnya, segera ia mengambil pakaiannya untuk menutupi dirinya. Pada saat itu orang-orang melihatnya dalam keadaan telanjang, ternyata pelir Nabi Musa tidak burut. (Dan adalah dia seorang yang mempunyai kedudukan terhormat di sisi Allah."). Di antara perlakuan yang menyakitkan Nabi saw. ialah pada suatu hari ia membagi-bagi ganimah, kemudian ada seorang lelaki berkata, "Ini adalah pembagian yang tidak berdasarkan keridaan Allah swt." Maka pada saat itu wajah Nabi saw. merah padam mendengar perkataan itu, lalu beliau segera bersabda, "Semoga Allah melimpahkan rahmat-Nya kepada Musa, sesungguhnya dia telah disakiti lebih dari ini tetapi dia tetap bersabar." Demikianlah menurut hadis yang diriwayatkan oleh Imam Bukhari.
t_id.iwq: Hai orang-orang yang beriman, janganlah kamu menjadi seperti orang-orang yang menyakiti Musa; maka Allah membersihkannya dari tuduhan-tuduhan yang mereka katakan. Dan adalah dia seorang yang mempunyai kedudukan terhormat di sisi Allah.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (يا أيها الذين آمنوا لا تكونوا) مع نبيكم (كالذين آذوا موسى) بقولهم مثلا: ما يمنعه أن يغتسل معنا إلا أنه آذر (فبرأه الله مما قالوا) بأن وضع ثوبه على حجر ليغتسل ففر الحجر به حتى وقف بين ملأ من بني إسرائيل فأدركه موسى فأخذ ثوبه فاستتر به فرأوه ولا أدرة به وهي نفخة في الخصية (وكان عند الله وجيها) ذا جاه: ومما أوذي به نبينا صلى الله عليه وسلم أنه قسم قسما فقال رجل هذه قسمة ما أريد بها وجه الله تعالى، فغضب النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم من ذلك وقال: "يرحم الله موسى لقد أوذي بأكثر من هذا فصبر" رواه البخاري
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تَكُونُواْ كَٱلَّذِينَ ءَاذَوۡاْ مُوسَىٰ فَبَرَّأَهُ ٱللَّهُ مِمَّا قَالُواْۚ وَكَانَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَجِيهٗا (وَجِيهًا: عَظِيمَ القَدْرِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Hai orang-orang yang beriman! Janganlah kalian menjadi) bersama Nabi kalian (seperti orang-orang yang menyakiti Musa) seperti perkataan mereka kepada Nabi Musa, "Sesungguhnya tidak ada yang mencegahnya untuk mandi bersama kita melainkan karena pelirnya burut (maka Allah membersihkannya dari tuduhan-tuduhan yang mereka katakan) yaitu pada suatu hari Nabi Musa ketika hendak mandi meletakkan pakaiannya pada sebuah batu, kemudian batu itu membawa lari pakaiannya, dan berhenti di tengah-tengah segolongan kaum Bani Israel. Lalu Nabi Musa mengejarnya dan dapat menyusulnya, segera ia mengambil pakaiannya untuk menutupi dirinya. Pada saat itu orang-orang melihatnya dalam keadaan telanjang, ternyata pelir Nabi Musa tidak burut. (Dan adalah dia seorang yang mempunyai kedudukan terhormat di sisi Allah."). Di antara perlakuan yang menyakitkan Nabi saw. ialah pada suatu hari ia membagi-bagi ganimah, kemudian ada seorang lelaki berkata, "Ini adalah pembagian yang tidak berdasarkan keridaan Allah swt." Maka pada saat itu wajah Nabi saw. merah padam mendengar perkataan itu, lalu beliau segera bersabda, "Semoga Allah melimpahkan rahmat-Nya kepada Musa, sesungguhnya dia telah disakiti lebih dari ini tetapi dia tetap bersabar." Demikianlah menurut hadis yang diriwayatkan oleh Imam Bukhari

ar: یَـٰۤأَیُّهَا ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَقُولُوا۟ قَوۡلࣰا سَدِیدࣰا
ar_simple: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا

t.sahih-int: O you who have believed, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice
t.asad: O you who have attained to faith! Remain conscious of God, and [always] speak with a will to bring out [only] what is just and true
t.yusuf-ali: O ye who believe! Fear Allah, and (always) say a word directed to the Right
t.pickthall: O ye who believe! Guard your duty to Allah, and speak words straight to the point
t.arberry: O believers, fear God, and speak words hitting the mark
t.hilali-khan: O you who believe! Keep your duty to Allah and fear Him, and speak (always) the truth
t.itani: O you who believe! Be conscious of God, and speak in a straightforward manner.
t.mubarakpuri: O you who believe! Have Taqwa of Allah and speak (always) the truth.
t.qarib: Believers, be mindful of God, and speak in a straightforward way
t.wahiduddin: Believers, fear God, and say the right word
t.shakir: O you who believe! be careful of (your duty to) Allah and speak the right word
t.haleem: Believers, be mindful of God, speak in a direct fashion and to good purpose
t.maariful: O you who believe, fear Allah, and speak in straightforward words
t.khattab: O believers! Be mindful of Allah, and say what is right
t.ghali: O you who have believed, be pious to Allah, and speak befitting words. (Literally: say a befitting saying)
t.irving: who believe, heed God and speak straight to the point
t.iwq: O you who believe, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice

t_id.kemenag: Hai orang-orang yang beriman, bertakwalah kamu kepada Allah dan katakanlah perkataan yang benar,
t_id.sabiq: Wahai orang-orang beriman, takutlah akan hukuman Allah jika kalian mendurhakai-Nya. Ucapkanlah perkataan yang benar dan lurus.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Hai orang-orang yang beriman! Bertakwalah kamu sekalian kepada Allah dan katakanlah perkataan yang benar) yakni perkataan yang tidak menyalahi.
t_id.iwq: Hai orang-orang yang beriman, bertakwalah kamu kepada Allah dan katakanlah perkataan yang benar,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا أيها الذين آمنوا اتقوا الله وقولوا قوْلا سديدا» صوابا
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ ٱتَّقُواْ ٱللَّهَ وَقُولُواْ قَوۡلٗا سَدِيدٗا (سَدِيدًا: مُوَافِقًا لَلحَقِّ، خَالِيًا مِنَ الكَذِبِ وَالبَاطِلِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Hai orang-orang yang beriman! Bertakwalah kamu sekalian kepada Allah dan katakanlah perkataan yang benar) yakni perkataan yang tidak menyalahi

ar: یُصۡلِحۡ لَكُمۡ أَعۡمَـٰلَكُمۡ وَیَغۡفِرۡ لَكُمۡ ذُنُوبَكُمۡۗ وَمَن یُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَقَدۡ فَازَ فَوۡزًا عَظِیمًا
ar_simple: يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ ۗ وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَقَدْ فَازَ فَوْزًا عَظِيمًا

t.sahih-int: He will [then] amend for you your deeds and forgive you your sins. And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger has certainly attained a great attainment
t.asad: [whereupon] He will cause your deeds to be virtuous, and will forgive you your sins. And [know that] whoever pays heed unto God and His Apostle has already attained to a mighty triumph
t.yusuf-ali: That He may make your conduct whole and sound and forgive you your sins: He that obeys Allah and His Messenger, has already attained the highest achievement
t.pickthall: He will adjust your works for you and will forgive you your sins. Whosoever obeyeth Allah and His messenger, he verily hath gained a signal victory
t.arberry: and He will set right your deeds for you and will forgive you your sins. Whosoever obeys God and His Messenger has won a mighty triumph
t.hilali-khan: He will direct you to do righteous good deeds and will forgive you your sins. And whosoever obeys Allah and His Messenger (SAW) he has indeed achieved a great achievement (i.e. he will be saved from the Hell-fire and made to enter Paradise)
t.itani: He will rectify your conduct for you, and will forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys God and His Messenger has won a great victory.
t.mubarakpuri: He will direct you to do righteous good deeds and will forgive you your sins. And whosoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, he has indeed achieved a great victory.
t.qarib: He will rectify your deeds and forgive you your sins. And whoever obeys God and His Messenger has certainly achieved a great success
t.wahiduddin: He will bless your works for you and forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys God and His Messenger has indeed achieved a great success
t.shakir: He will put your deeds into a right state for you, and forgive you your faults; and whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, he indeed achieves a mighty success
t.haleem: and He will put your deeds right for you and forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys God and His Messenger will truly achieve a great triumph
t.maariful: (If you do so,) Allah will correct your deeds for your benefit, and forgive your sins for you. Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger achieves a great success
t.khattab: He will bless your deeds for you, and forgive your sins. And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, has truly achieved a great triumph
t.ghali: (Then) He will make your deeds righteous for you, and He will forgive you your guilty (deeds); and whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, then he has already triumphed a magnificent triumph
t.irving: He will improve your actions for you and forgive you your sins. Anyone who obeys God and His messenger will achieve a splendid Triumph. God´s Trust; The Human Dilemma
t.iwq: He will then amend for you your deeds and forgive you your sins. And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger has certainly attained a great attainment

t_id.kemenag: niscaya Allah memperbaiki bagimu amalan-amalanmu dan mengampuni bagimu dosa-dosamu. Dan barangsiapa mentaati Allah dan Rasul-Nya, maka sesungguhnya ia telah mendapat kemenangan yang besar.
t_id.sabiq: Niscaya Allah akan memberikan perkenan-Nya bagi kalian untuk melakukan kebajikan-kebajikan dan menghapuskan dosa kalian. Siapa saja yang takut pada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, sungguh telah mendapatkan kemenangan yang besar, selamat dari hukuman dan akan mendapatkan pahala.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Niscaya Allah memperbaiki bagi kalian amal-amal kalian) yakni Dia menerimanya (dan mengampuni bagi kalian dosa-dosa kalian. Siapa yang menaati Allah dan Rasul-Nya, maka sesungguhnya ia telah mendapatkan kemenangan yang besar) yaitu dia telah memperoleh apa yang paling didambakannya.
t_id.iwq: niscaya Allah memperbaiki bagimu amalan-amalanmu dan mengampuni bagimu dosa-dosamu. Dan barangsiapa mentaati Allah dan Rasul-Nya, maka sesungguhnya ia telah mendapat kemenangan yang besar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يصلح لكم أعمالكم» يتقبلها «ويغفر لكم ذنوبكم ومن يطع الله ورسوله فقد فاز فوزا عظيما» نال غاية مطلوبة
tafsir.siraj-ar: يُصۡلِحۡ لَكُمۡ أَعۡمَٰلَكُمۡ وَيَغۡفِرۡ لَكُمۡ ذُنُوبَكُمۡۗ وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَقَدۡ فَازَ فَوۡزًا عَظِيمًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Niscaya Allah memperbaiki bagi kalian amal-amal kalian) yakni Dia menerimanya (dan mengampuni bagi kalian dosa-dosa kalian. Siapa yang menaati Allah dan Rasul-Nya, maka sesungguhnya ia telah mendapatkan kemenangan yang besar) yaitu dia telah memperoleh apa yang paling didambakannya

ar: إِنَّا عَرَضۡنَا ٱلۡأَمَانَةَ عَلَى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَٱلۡجِبَالِ فَأَبَیۡنَ أَن یَحۡمِلۡنَهَا وأَشۡفَقۡنَ مِنۡهَا وَحَمَلَهَا ٱلۡإِنسَـٰنُۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ ظَلُومࣰا جَهُولࣰا
ar_simple: إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا الْأَمَانَةَ عَلَى السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالْجِبَالِ فَأَبَيْنَ أَنْ يَحْمِلْنَهَا وَأَشْفَقْنَ مِنْهَا وَحَمَلَهَا الْإِنْسَانُ ۖ إِنَّهُ كَانَ ظَلُومًا جَهُولًا

t.sahih-int: Indeed, we offered the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, and they declined to bear it and feared it; but man [undertook to] bear it. Indeed, he was unjust and ignorant
t.asad: Verily, We did offer the trust [of reason and volition] to the heavens, and the earth, and the mountains: but they refused to bear it because they were afraid of it. Yet man took it up - for, verily, he has always been prone to be most wicked, most foolish
t.yusuf-ali: We did indeed offer the Trust to the Heavens and the Earth and the Mountains; but they refused to undertake it, being afraid thereof: but man undertook it;- He was indeed unjust and foolish
t.pickthall: Lo! We offered the trust unto the heavens and the earth and the hills, but they shrank from bearing it and were afraid of it. And man assumed it. Lo! he hath proved a tyrant and a fool
t.arberry: We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to carry it and were afraid of it; and man carried it. Surely he is sinful, very foolish
t.hilali-khan: Truly, We did offer AlAmanah (the trust or moral responsibility or honesty and all the duties which Allah has ordained) to the heavens and the earth, and the mountains, but they declined to bear it and were afraid of it (i.e. afraid of Allah's Torment). But man bore it. Verily, he was unjust (to himself) and ignorant (of its results)
t.itani: We offered the Trust to the heavens, and the earth, and the mountains; but they refused to bear it, and were apprehensive of it; but the human being accepted it. He was unfair and ignorant.
t.mubarakpuri: Truly, We did offer the Amanah to the heavens and the earth, and the mountains, but they declined to bear it and were afraid of it. But man bore it. Verily, he was unjust and ignorant.
t.qarib: We offered the Trust to the heavens, the earth and the mountains; but they refused to bear it and feared it, yet the human being took it on. He has always been unjust and ignorant
t.wahiduddin: We offered the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear it, because they were afraid of it. But man bore it: he surely proved unjust and ignorant
t.shakir: Surely We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to be unfaithful to it and feared from it, and man has turned unfaithful to it; surely he is unjust, ignorant
t.haleem: We offered the Trust to the heavens, the earth, and the mountains, yet they refused to undertake it and were afraid of it; mankind undertook it- they have always been inept and foolish
t.maariful: We did offer the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear its burden and were afraid of it, and man picked it up. Indeed he is unjust (to himself), unaware (of the end)
t.khattab: Indeed, We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they ˹all˺ declined to bear it, being fearful of it. But humanity assumed it, ˹for˺ they are truly wrongful ˹to themselves˺ and ignorant ˹of the consequences˺
t.ghali: Surely We presented (Literally: set before) the Trust (i.e., Trust of devotion) to the heavens and the earth and mountains. Yet they refused to carry it and felt timorous about it, and man carried it. Surely he has been constantly unjust, constantly ignorant
t.irving: We offered the trust to Heaven and Earth, and to the mountains too, yet they refused to carry it, and shrank back from it. However man accepted it: he has been unfair [to himself], ignorant
t.iwq: Indeed, we offered the Trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, and they declined to bear it and feared it; but man undertook to bear it. Indeed, he was unjust and ignorant

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Kami telah mengemukakan amanat kepada langit, bumi dan gunung-gunung, maka semuanya enggan untuk memikul amanat itu dan mereka khawatir akan mengkhianatinya, dan dipikullah amanat itu oleh manusia. Sesungguhnya manusia itu amat zalim dan amat bodoh,
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah menawarkan kepada bumi, langit dan gunung untuk mengemban tugas-tugas keagamaan. Tapi mereka tidak bersedia melaksanakan misi itu karena takut. Tetapi manusia menyanggupinya. Sungguh manusia itu sangat zalim pada diri sendiri dan tidak mengetahui kemampuan dirinya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami telah mengemukakan amanat) yaitu ibadah salat dan ibadah-ibadah lainnya, apabila dikerjakan, pelakunya akan mendapat pahala, dan apabila ditinggalkan, pelakunya akan disiksa (pada langit, bumi dan gunung-gunung) seumpamanya Allah menciptakan pada masing-masing pemahaman dan dapat berbicara (maka semuanya enggan untuk memikul amanat itu dan mereka khawatir) yakni merasa takut (akan mengkhianatinya lalu dipikullah amanat itu oleh manusia) oleh Nabi Adam, sesudah terlebih dahulu ditawarkan kepadanya. (Sesungguhnya manusia itu amat zalim) terhadap dirinya sendiri, disebabkan apa yang telah dipikulnya itu (lagi amat bodoh) tidak mengerti tentang apa yang dipikulnya itu.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Kami telah mengemukakan amanat kepada langit, bumi dan gunung-gunung, maka semuanya enggan untuk memikul amanat itu dan mereka khawatir akan mengkhianatinya, dan dipikullah amanat itu oleh manusia. Sesungguhnya manusia itu amat zalim dan amat bodoh,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا عرضنا الأمانة» الصلوات وغيرهما مما في فعلها من الثواب وتركها من العقاب «على السماوات والأرض والجبال» بأن خلق فيهما فهما ونطقا «فأبين أن يحملنها وأشفقن» خفن «منها وحملها الإنسان» آدم بعد عرضها عليه «إنه كان ظلوما» لنفسه بما حمله «جهولا» به
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّا عَرَضۡنَا ٱلۡأَمَانَةَ عَلَى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَٱلۡجِبَالِ فَأَبَيۡنَ أَن يَحۡمِلۡنَهَا وَأَشۡفَقۡنَ مِنۡهَا وَحَمَلَهَا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ ظَلُومٗا جَهُولٗا (الْأَمَانَةَ: مَا أَمَرَ اللهُ بِهِ، وَنَهَى عَنْهُ, فَأَبَيْنَ: امْتَنَعْنَ, وَأَشْفَقْنَ: خِفْنَ مِنَ الخِيَانَةِ فِيهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami telah mengemukakan amanat) yaitu ibadah salat dan ibadah-ibadah lainnya, apabila dikerjakan, pelakunya akan mendapat pahala, dan apabila ditinggalkan, pelakunya akan disiksa (pada langit, bumi dan gunung-gunung) seumpamanya Allah menciptakan pada masing-masing pemahaman dan dapat berbicara (maka semuanya enggan untuk memikul amanat itu dan mereka khawatir) yakni merasa takut (akan mengkhianatinya lalu dipikullah amanat itu oleh manusia) oleh Nabi Adam, sesudah terlebih dahulu ditawarkan kepadanya. (Sesungguhnya manusia itu amat zalim) terhadap dirinya sendiri, disebabkan apa yang telah dipikulnya itu (lagi amat bodoh) tidak mengerti tentang apa yang dipikulnya itu

ar: لِّیُعَذِّبَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلۡمُنَـٰفِقِینَ وَٱلۡمُنَـٰفِقَـٰتِ وَٱلۡمُشۡرِكِینَ وَٱلۡمُشۡرِكَـٰتِ وَیَتُوبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَى ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِینَ وَٱلۡمُؤۡمِنَـٰتِۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورࣰا رَّحِیمَۢا
ar_simple: لِيُعَذِّبَ اللَّهُ الْمُنَافِقِينَ وَالْمُنَافِقَاتِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ وَالْمُشْرِكَاتِ وَيَتُوبَ اللَّهُ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا

t.sahih-int: [It was] so that Allah may punish the hypocrite men and hypocrite women and the men and women who associate others with Him and that Allah may accept repentance from the believing men and believing women. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful
t.asad: [And so it is] that God imposes suffering on the hypocrites, both men and women, as well as on the men and women who ascribe divinity to aught beside Him. And [so, too, it is] that God turns in His mercy unto the believing men and believing women: for God is indeed much-forgiving, a dispenser of grace
t.yusuf-ali: (With the result) that Allah has to punish the Hypocrites, men and women, and the Unbelievers, men and women, and Allah turns in Mercy to the Believers, men and women: for Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful
t.pickthall: So Allah punisheth hypocritical men and hypocritical women, and idolatrous men and idolatrous women. But Allah pardoneth believing men and believing women, and Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful
t.arberry: That God may chastise the hypocrites, men and women alike, and the idolaters, men and women alike; and that God may turn again unto the believers, men and women alike. God is All-forgiving, All-compassionate
t.hilali-khan: So that Allah will punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the men and women who are AlMushrikun (the polytheists, idolaters, pagans, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, and in His Messenger Muhammad SAW). And Allah will pardon (accept the repentance of) the true believers of the Islamic Monotheism, men and women. And Allah is Ever OftForgiving, Most Merciful
t.itani: God will punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the idolaters, men and women. And God will redeem the believers, men and women. God is Ever-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
t.mubarakpuri: So that Allah will punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the men and women who are idolators. And Allah will pardon the believers, men and women. And Allah is Ever Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
t.qarib: [This is in order that] God punishes the hypocrites, men and women, as well as the men and women who associate others with Him, and why God turns mercifully towards the believing men and women, for God is the Ever-Forgiving, the Mercy Giver
t.wahiduddin: God will punish the hypocrites, both men and women, and polytheists, both men and women, but God will turn in His mercy to believing men and believing women; God is most forgiving and most merciful
t.shakir: So Allah will chastise the hypocritical men and the hypocritical women and the polytheistic men and the polytheistic women, and Allah will turn (mercifully) to the believing women, and Allah is Forgiving, Merciful
t.haleem: God will punish the hypocrites and the idolaters, both men and women, and turn with mercy to the believers, both men and women: God is most forgiving, most merciful
t.maariful: The result (of all this) is that Allah will punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the Mushriks, men and women, and will accept the repentance of the believing men and women. Surely Allah is Most-Forgiving, Very-Merciful
t.khattab: so that Allah will punish hypocrite men and women and polytheistic men and women, and Allah will turn in mercy to believing men and women. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful
t.ghali: That Allah may torment the male hypocrites and the female hypocrites, and the male associators and the female associators, (i.e., those who associate orhers with Allah) and that Allah may relent towards the male believers and the female believers; and Allah has been Ever-Forgiving, Ever-Merciful
t.irving: Still God will punish hypocritical men and hypocritical women and associating men and associating women. However God relents with believing men and believing women. God is Forgiving, Merciful
t.iwq: It was so that Allah may punish the hypocrite men and hypocrite women and the men and women who associate others with Him and that Allah may accept repentance from the believing men and believing women. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful

t_id.kemenag: sehingga Allah mengazab orang-orang munafik laki-laki dan perempuan dan orang-orang musyrikin laki-laki dan perempuan; dan sehingga Allah menerima taubat orang-orang mukmin laki-laki dan perempuan. Dan adalah Allah Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.
t_id.sabiq: Agar Allah menjatuhkan siksa atas orang-orang munafik, baik laki-laki maupun wanita, dan orang-orang musyrk, baik laki-laki maupun wanita. Juga agar Allah menerima pertobatan orang-orang beriman, baik laki-laki maupun wanita. Allah Mahabanyak ampunan-Nya dan Mahaluas kasih sayang-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sehingga Allah mengazab) huruf Lam berta'alluq kepada lafal 'Aradhnaa, sebagai akibat dari apa yang telah dipikul oleh Nabi Adam (orang-orang munafik laki-laki dan perempuan dan orang-orang musyrik laki-laki dan perempuan) yakni orang-orang yang menyia-nyiakan amanat itu (dan sehingga Allah menerima tobat orang-orang Mukmin laki-laki dan perempuan) yaitu orang-orang yang menunaikan amanahnya. (Dan adalah Allah Maha Pengampun) kepada orang-orang Mukmin (lagi Maha Penyayang) kepada mereka.
t_id.iwq: sehingga Allah mengazab orang-orang munafik laki-laki dan perempuan dan orang-orang musyrikin laki-laki dan perempuan; dan sehingga Allah menerima taubat orang-orang mukmin laki-laki dan perempuan. Dan adalah Allah Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ليعذب الله» اللام متعلقة بعرضنا المترتب عليه حمل آدم «المنافقين والمنافقات والمشركين والمشركات» المضيعين الأمانة «ويتوب الله على المؤمنين والمؤمنات» المؤدين الأمانة «وكان الله غفورا» للمؤمنين «رحيما» بهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: لِّيُعَذِّبَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلۡمُنَٰفِقِينَ وَٱلۡمُنَٰفِقَٰتِ وَٱلۡمُشۡرِكِينَ وَٱلۡمُشۡرِكَٰتِ وَيَتُوبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَى ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ وَٱلۡمُؤۡمِنَٰتِۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورٗا رَّحِيمَۢا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sehingga Allah mengazab) huruf Lam berta'alluq kepada lafal 'Aradhnaa, sebagai akibat dari apa yang telah dipikul oleh Nabi Adam (orang-orang munafik laki-laki dan perempuan dan orang-orang musyrik laki-laki dan perempuan) yakni orang-orang yang menyia-nyiakan amanat itu (dan sehingga Allah menerima tobat orang-orang Mukmin laki-laki dan perempuan) yaitu orang-orang yang menunaikan amanahnya. (Dan adalah Allah Maha Pengampun) kepada orang-orang Mukmin (lagi Maha Penyayang) kepada mereka
